LiDAR Settings

Relaunch — LiDAR

Workspace: ~/next_ros2
Front: ros2 launch sllidar_ros2 sllidar_t1_launch.py
Rear: ros2 launch sllidar_ros2 sllidar_s2e_launch.py

Relaunch — IMU

Workspace: ~/next_EKF

Topic Mappings
About
Configure UI data mappings and ROS topic/service/action endpoints. Values are saved to YAML.
Action node mappings use keys prefixed with action_node_ (e.g. action_node_lift_up). Extend actions via window.CoreActions.registerAction({ id, label, topic, type, field }).
UI Mappings
Mapping Key Topic Field Type
ROS Endpoints
Endpoint Key Name
Map Stitching — Extend Existing Map
How map stitching works:
slam_toolbox stores a pose graph (not just a .pgm image). Loading the old pose graph lets the robot localize itself into the existing map geometry, then continue extending it with new scans. The result is one unified, consistent map.

1 Load Existing Pose Graph

Point to the .posegraph file saved by a previous SLAM session. slam_toolbox will deserialize it and the robot will localize into that map.

2 Start Continued / Lifelong Mapping

Start the robot in a place that overlaps the old map. SLAM will match incoming scans to the existing pose graph and extend it as you drive into new areas.

3 Save Extended Map

When done driving, serialize the extended pose graph and save the new .pgm/.yaml map for navigation.

slam_toolbox services used: /slam_toolbox/deserialize_map  |  /slam_toolbox/serialize_map  |  /initialpose (for relocalization)
